title: Probabilistic Programming, in Practice
tagline: PyMC / NumPyro / Stan are the same idea — declare a model, hand it to an inference engine
summary: Write a Bayesian model as code; sample it with HMC; check r-hat and ESS; compare with VI. A live coin-bias model + a 2-D regression run NUTS-flavour MCMC and a Gaussian VI in your browser, side-by-side, with trace plots and the diagnostics you'd see in PyMC / NumPyro / Stan.
highlights: PPL recipe: declare model, choose inference (HMC / VI), check diagnostics.
Live coin-bias model: HMC trace + posterior histogram + analytic Beta posterior.
2-D Bayesian regression: HMC vs VI fit on a small dataset.
r-hat, ESS, divergent transitions, posterior predictive checks — what each is for.
